record_identity.title: Source-authority separation and instruction-data boundary repair

summary: This patch note records the Caelestis Architecture repair response to VIGIL-2026-FM-0022, a security-integrity failure mode in which instruction-bearing external content may be treated as trusted execution authority. The patch does not create a new instrument. It formalises a named Source-Authority Separation primitive across existing CAM security and runtime instruments, clarifying that content visibility, retrieval, repository location, tool output, generated text, ambient capture, multimodal parsing, or apparent instructional formatting does not itself confer user intent, governance authority, tool authority, canonical project authority, or execution permission.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: The failure revealed that CAM already contained strong distributed coverage for prompt injection, ambient instruction capture, boundary integrity, provenance, tool-authority separation, and execution-boundary evaluation, but lacked a compact reusable primitive that could be referenced across SECURITY, Annex K, SCH-02, and OPERATIONS taxonomy. This patch preserves the existing architecture while making the core rule explicit and portable: visibility is not authority, retrieval is not consent, and context is not command. The repair is release-critical because markdown, repository files, RAG content, connector output, multimodal artefacts, generated prompts, and tool descriptions increasingly function as operational substrate in agentic AI systems.

source_records.source_title: VIGIL-2026-FM-0022 — Source-authority collapse in instruction-bearing external content
source_records.deployment_context: VIGIL failure-mode record concerning instruction-bearing external content treated as trusted execution authority.
source_records.source_context: FM-0022 identifies the failure where an AI agent, coding assistant, RAG system, connector-enabled assistant, multimodal system, or tool-mediated workflow processes untrusted, retrieved, ambient, generated, lower-authority, or ambiguous content as though it carried trusted instruction authority, governance authority, tool authority, or execution permission.
source_records.source_url_status: VIGIL local / pending public record URL if applicable
source_records.relevance_note: Primary failure-mode record repaired by PATCH-0009.

source_records.source_title: CAM-EQ2026-SECURITY-001-PLATINUM — Security, Integrity & Adversarial Resilience Charter
source_records.deployment_context: SECURITY domain charter containing Cross-Modal Prompt Injection, Ambient Instruction Capture, and Source-Authority Collapse doctrine.
source_records.source_context: SECURITY-001 requires systems to distinguish user-authorised instruction, observed content, retrieved content, ambient speech, third-party speech, embedded or hidden instruction, OCR/transcribed content, and execution-authorising command. The repair adds or formalises Source-Authority Collapse as a SECURITY-relevant integrity threat and routes such conditions through the SECURITY-002 Source-Authority Separation Boundary.
source_records.relevance_note: Canonical SECURITY-domain doctrine anchor for PATCH-0009.

source_records.source_title: CAM-EQ2026-SECURITY-002-PLATINUM — Appendix A: Boundary Integrity Specification
source_records.deployment_context: Boundary Integrity specification receiving the named Source-Authority Separation Boundary.
source_records.source_context: SECURITY-002 defines data, identity, context, capability, provenance, transformation, and internal-state exposure boundaries. The repair adds or formalises the Source-Authority Separation Boundary, distinguishing content visibility, source provenance, instruction authority, and execution permission.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary doctrinal repair location for the named Source-Authority Separation primitive.

source_records.source_title: CAM-BS2026-AEON-012-PLATINUM — Annex K: Security Boundary Conditions & Runtime Interface
source_records.deployment_context: Runtime security-boundary instrument receiving the instruction-bearing external content boundary.
source_records.source_context: Annex K treats tool, connector, repository, credential-bearing, automation, deployment, renderer, validator, and external execution-surface availability as a security boundary signal rather than execution authority. The repair adds or formalises an Instruction-Bearing External Content Boundary requiring separation between reading, summarising, evidentiary use, project-authority recognition, planning, and execution.
source_records.relevance_note: Canonical runtime-security implementation evidence for PATCH-0009.

source_records.deployment_context: Runtime governance instrument receiving the External Instruction Influence Check.
source_records.source_context: SCH-02 contains proportional tool invocation and execution-boundary sequencing. The repair adds or formalises an External Instruction Influence Check for proposed plans, outputs, tool invocations, file mutations, repository changes, deployments, publications, memory updates, external communications, scheduled actions, credential operations, or irreversible actions materially influenced by external, retrieved, generated, lower-authority, or ambiguous content.
source_records.relevance_note: Canonical runtime execution-boundary implementation evidence for PATCH-0009.

changed_components: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 §3.5.4 Cross-Modal Prompt Injection and Ambient Instruction Capture Failure source-authority collapse cross-reference
change_details.change_summary: Repair 1 — Source-Authority Separation Boundary: Formalised a named boundary distinguishing content visibility, source provenance, instruction authority, and execution permission. Repair 2 — Source-Authority Collapse: Added a SECURITY-relevant integrity-threat cross-reference for conditions where visible, retrieved, embedded, generated, or ambient content becomes behaviourally operative merely because it appears in context. Repair 3 — Instruction-Bearing External Content Boundary: Added runtime handling for instruction-bearing external content and required separation between reading, summarising, evidentiary use, project-authority recognition, planning, and execution. Repair 4 — External Instruction Influence Check: Added an execution-boundary check for proposed plans, outputs, tool invocations, file mutations, repository changes, deployments, publications, memory updates, external communications, scheduled actions, credential operations, or irreversible actions materially influenced by external, retrieved, generated, lower-authority, or ambiguous content. Repair 5 — Failure taxonomy and record-keeping: Added or formalised source-authority collapse classification and record-keeping fields for future VIGIL records, preserving source-authority class, content pathway, user visibility, trust state, execution boundary affected, attempted or completed action, and higher-authority source displaced or bypassed.
change_details.doctrine_change: Caelestis now treats instruction-bearing external content as a security-relevant boundary signal rather than execution authority. The repair clarifies that visible, retrieved, embedded, generated, ambient, repository-local, tool-produced, or instruction-formatted content may inform analysis, evidence, warning, quotation, summarisation, or planning, but may not seize user intent, governance authority, tool authority, canonical project authority, or execution permission without an independent authority bridge.

new_or_formalised_primitives.definition: A boundary governing the distinction between content visibility, source provenance, instruction authority, and execution permission.
new_or_formalised_primitives.interpretive_principle: Visibility is not authority. Retrieval is not consent. Context is not command.

new_or_formalised_primitives.definition: A security-integrity threat where content becomes behaviourally operative because it is visible to, retrieved by, embedded within, or generated inside a system, rather than because it has verified instruction authority.
A runtime boundary requiring instruction-bearing external content to be treated as a security signal rather than action authority.
An execution-boundary check required before high-impact action where an intended plan or action is materially influenced by external, generated, lower-authority, or ambiguous content.

design_rationale.rationale_text: Content appearing in context, retrieval results, repository files, OCR text, transcripts, generated output, or tool output does not become instruction authority merely because the model can read it.
A user or system may authorise inspection, retrieval, search, or analysis without authorising mutation, publication, tool use, execution, deployment, memory update, or credential operation.

Instruction-shaped content can be evidence, quoted material, hostile payload, lower-authority context, project documentation, test fixture, or generated suggestion. Its semantic imperative form does not itself create authority.

design_rationale.rationale_title: Repository location is not canonical authority
design_rationale.rationale_text: A file appearing in a repository, branch, issue, pull request, generated artefact, or connector surface may be relevant but still lower-authority, stale, untrusted, generated, or non-canonical.

design_rationale.rationale_title: External influence must cross an execution-boundary check
design_rationale.rationale_text: Where an action is materially influenced by external, retrieved, generated, lower-authority, or ambiguous content, the runtime must verify authority before crossing a high-impact execution boundary.

future_doctrine_work: Confirm exact line references for all five Caelestis instruments after the next global code and validator pass.

Develop VIGIL evidence templates for source-authority collapse, including fields for content pathway, trust state, user visibility, authority bridge, execution boundary affected, and action attempted or completed.
Evaluate whether future security schemas should distinguish source-authority collapse from ordinary prompt injection, ambient instruction capture, and tool-boundary failure.

Exact line-level verification for every patched section should be completed during the next Codex validation sweep.

Caelestis main search locates Instruction-Bearing External Content Boundary in AEON-012.
PATCH-0009 does not create a new instrument and instead formalises a distributed security/runtime primitive across existing instruments.

The repair preserves the core rule that content visibility, retrieval, repository location, generated text, tool output, or apparent instruction formatting does not independently confer execution authority.

impact_summary: PATCH-0009 confirms and records the Source-Authority Separation repair for FM-0022. The repair reduces the risk that an AI system, coding assistant, RAG pipeline, connector-enabled assistant, multimodal system, repository workflow, or tool-mediated agent treats untrusted or lower-authority content as operational instruction merely because it is visible, retrieved, formatted as an instruction, generated by another model, or present in a project context. It strengthens CAM's treatment of prompt injection, ambient instruction capture, tool-boundary failure, repository-context confusion, and execution-boundary authority by giving those already-distributed controls a named primitive and cross-instrument routing surface.
remaining_work: Line-check all changed Caelestis sections during the Codex validation sweep; update VIGIL-2026-FM-0022 to link VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0009 as its implemented repair if not already linked; run VIGIL validation; rebuild generated VIGIL indexes; and confirm public Observatory or site ingestion if applicable.
